rulings.Case Title: SAMSARA INC. VS. MOTIVE TECHNOLOGIES, INC,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 23, 2026, line 7. PETITIONER SAMSARA INC.'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Petitioner Samsara Inc.'s application for the pro hac vice admission of Brian Rosenthal is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. The court will sign the proposed order previously submitted by Samsara. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ABDELMAJID SABOUR VS. RENEE FLORES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Clarifying Prior Court Order Of December 19, 2024, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Staying The Entire Action Until Plaintiff And The Uberrelated Defendants Complete Binding Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Norman L. Pearl
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 23, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANTS RENEE FLORES, VISUAL EDGE TECHNOLOGY INC'S Motion For Order Clarifying Prior Court Order Of December 19, 2024, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Staying The Entire Action Until Plaintiff And The Uberrelated Defendants Complete Binding Arbitration. Defendants Renee Flores and Visual Edge Technology, Inc.'s motion for order clarifying prior court order of December 19, 2024 is granted. The order of December 19, 2024 stayed the entirety of this action until the completion of arbitration proceedings between plaintiff Abdelmajid Sabour and the Uber defendants. The Uber defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay action contended that the arbitration agreements between them and Sabour provided that the "Federal Arbitration Act will govern" the "interpretation and enforcement" of the parties' arbitration agreements. (Uber Memo at 10:2-4). Unlike the California Arbitration Act which the parties correctly point out does not require stay of the entirety of a case when an issue therein is arbitrable, section 3 of the Federal Arbitration Act "provides that, when any issue in a suit is subject to arbitration, the court 'shall on application of one of the parties stay the trial of the action until such arbitration has been had...'" (Smith v. Spizzirri (2024) 601 US 472, 476). Thus, in accordance with the FAA's mandatory stay of the entirety of a case when an issue therein is determined to be arbitrable, the December 19, 2024 order requires the stay of the entirety of this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC VS. MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Non-Compliance Of Court Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for April 23, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC'S Motion For Sanctions For Non-Compliance Of Court Order. Plaintiff Kapolei Energy Storage I, LLC's motion for sanctions for non-compliance of court order is granted in small part. No later than May 8, 2026 cross-defendant Electric Power Systems, International Inc. must pay $5,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions for its pattern of discovery abuse by failing to comply with the order filed March 13, 2026. These sanctions are authorized by CCP 2023.030 per City of Los Angeles v. PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP (2024) 17 Cal. 5th 46, 74-75. KES' request for $60,000 is excessive in two ways. First, it appears to be based in part on costs it claimed to have incurred to obtain the order filed March 13, 2026, yet the order resolved the issue of KES' entitlement to costs to obtain that order. Second, while EPS' pattern of discovery abuse is troubling, there is an insufficient showing that the amount KES seeks is commensurate with the harm it has suffered due to EPS' discovery abuse since the issuance of the March 13, 2026 order or that the amount sought is necessary to induce EPS to comply with its discovery obligations in the future. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
